or **facility identifier**, is a unique integer number[[1]](#cite_note-fcc-help_pa-1) of one to six digits,[[2]](#cite_note-2) assigned by the U.S. [Federal Communications Commission](/wiki/Federal_Communications_Commission "Federal Communications Commission") (FCC) Media Bureau[[1]](#cite_note-fcc-help_pa-1) to each [broadcast station](/wiki/Radio_broadcasting "Radio broadcasting") in the FCC Consolidated Database System (CDBS) and Licensing and Management System (LMS) databases, among others.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Unique number for broadcast station in the United States

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Facility_ID "Special:EditPage/Facility ID") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Facility ID"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Facility+ID%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Facility+ID%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Facility+ID%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Facility+ID%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Facility+ID%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Facility+ID%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2009)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The **facility ID number**, also called a **FIN** or **facility identifier**, is a unique integer number[[1]](#cite_note-fcc-help_pa-1) of one to six digits,[[2]](#cite_note-2) assigned by the U.S. [Federal Communications Commission](/wiki/Federal_Communications_Commission "Federal Communications Commission") (FCC) Media Bureau[[1]](#cite_note-fcc-help_pa-1) to each [broadcast station](/wiki/Radio_broadcasting "Radio broadcasting") in the FCC Consolidated Database System (CDBS) and Licensing and Management System (LMS) databases, among others.

Because CDBS includes information about foreign stations which are notified to the U.S. under the terms of international [frequency coordination](/wiki/Frequency_coordination "Frequency coordination") agreements, FINs are also assigned to affected foreign stations. However, this has no legal significance, and the numbers are not used by the [regulatory](/wiki/Regulation "Regulation") authorities in those other countries.

Current FCC practice is to assign facility ID numbers sequentially, but this is not an official requirement, so third-party users must not rely on it. Unlike [call signs](/wiki/Call_signs_in_North_America "Call signs in North America"), however, the FIN associated with a particular station never changes; thus, the FCC staff and interested parties can be certain to which station an application pertains, even if it has changed its [call sign](/wiki/Call_sign "Call sign") since the application was originally filed. (The previous FCC database system, the Broadcast Application Processing System or BAPS, did not have such an identifier.)

In several cases, television stations have swapped facilities, and thus their FIN numbers, as what occurred in 1995 in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami"), when [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")-owned station [WTVJ](/wiki/WTVJ "WTVJ") swapped channels with [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS")'s WCIX-TV (after the swap, [WFOR-TV](/wiki/WFOR-TV "WFOR-TV")); NBC thus took the FIN and transmitter formerly associated with WCIX-TV, while WFOR-TV continues to operate under the FIN originally established for WTVJ.
